we recently stayed at the hilton garden inn on w
th st
in manhattan and we couldnt be happier with our choice the location is simply unbeatable
just a short walk from penn station we found it incredibly easy to navigate the city with public transportation right at our fingertips
the area is bustling with life filled with a fantastic array of restaurants food carts and shopping options with macys a stone throw away
being so close to madison square garden and the iconic empire state building was a real treat
in fact we were lucky enough to have a room with a stunning view of the empire state building – it made for some unforgettable moments
as for the rooms they are standard for a hilton garden inn which means they are on the smaller side as expected in nyc but they were impeccably clean and wellmaintained
the amenities were average but that didn’t detract from our experience
one thing to note is that while the hotel doesnt have a fullfledged restaurant the breakfast option they offered was quite good albeit a bit different from the typical hilton garden inn spread
there’s also a bar that operates during the week though it’s closed on weekends which was a slight letdown for us
the outdoor seating area looked cozy and inviting perfect for a relaxing moment between adventures
we also appreciated the gym in the basement which allowed us to keep up with our fitness routines
plus the pantry near the front desk was stocked with all sorts of goodies for snacks and essentials
the staff were incredibly helpful and attentive making our stay all the more enjoyable
given how busy the hotel was we were impressed by how well they managed everything
they even arranged a car service to pick us up for our flight
overall we truly enjoyed our stay at the hilton garden inn on w
th st
and would highly recommend it to anyone looking for a comfortable and conveniently located spot in the heart of manhattan

great location but with typical nyc room size limitations
the hilton garden inn on west th street boasts a superb location
its right across from macys herald square and near multiple subway lines making everything in nyc easily accessible
the hotel itself has a wellstocked shop in the reception area which was a lifesaver for grabbing midnight snacks
the staff was friendly and helpful providing everything i needed during my stay
however its important to be aware that this is a midrange hotel with typical new york city room limitations
the rooms are on the smaller side which is common for the city
additionally while there is a microwave there werent any microwavesafe dishes available to heat up food
the hvac unit also lacked a fanonly option which made it chilly at times since i couldnt adjust it to just circulate air
overall this hotel is a solid choice for budgetminded travelers who prioritize location and convenience over spacious accommodations and highend amenities

we usually don’t leave reviews but we felt it was important to share our experience to help future guests better understand what they might encounter during their stay
first and foremost the location was absolutely stunning
we stayed in a room on the nd floor which offered breathtaking views of the river—a definite highlight of our trip
however there were several issues that impacted our overall experience
upon arrival the staff was unable to locate our reservation which caused unnecessary stress after a long flight from california
thankfully they eventually found it but the initial confusion was unsettling
the room itself was uncomfortably stuffy and the air conditioning struggled to maintain a comfortable temperature throughout our stay making it difficult to relax
attempts to contact the front desk from our room were frustratingly unsuccessful
no one answered the phone and messages sent through the app went unanswered
we had to go directly to the front desk to address any concerns
unfortunately no room service was provided during our stay
this left us with unmade beds overflowing trash cans wet towels on the floor and empty body wash dispensers which was disappointing
even after speaking directly with housekeeping and the front desk room service was still not provided
instead we were handed towels to carry up ourselves and given two small bars of soap as a substitute which felt inadequate
we were only informed about the credits available to us on the night before checkout leaving us scrambling to use them lastminute at the gift shop for souvenirs
overall the staff appeared indifferent and not particularly interested in assisting guests
we hope this feedback helps improve the experience for future visitors as the location truly has so much potential

eventually i went down to the front desk where they acknowledged the request had been shared they provided towels from the back

after a long day and a late check‑in we were simply hoping for a nice shower and rest
additional concerns included a heaterair conditioner that didn’t work—the fan never turned on—and a toilet that ran continuously
the shower head’s water setting couldn’t be adjusted and there were no hand towels provided for initial preroom service

since we were only staying one more night we chose not to call in the maintenance issues
despite the inconveniences the harbor view and the hotel’s location were genuinely enjoyable and made exploring the area easy
with some improvements to service responsiveness and room maintenance this could be a much better experience

we stayed at this hotel on

at approximately am we along with everyone else staying in the hotel were awoken to a blaring alarmsiren with an automated message indicating there had been an emergency on site and to remain on standby
minutes go by and the same alarm and message again are blared over the speaker system
after about an hour had passed from the initial alarm and no update had been provided i called the front desk to see what was happening and was told there was no emergency
a drunk belligerent guest had set off the alarm and that was all

this was alarming and unnerving
we did not know whether we should evacuate or stay in our room because there was literally no further update or instruction from the hotel management or staff
at no point did the hotel update us make an announcement that there was no emergency apologize or make any attempt to restitute the situation
if we didn’t call the front desk we would have had no idea what was really happening
to say we were shocked is an understatement
whoever the standing manager was at the time should be relieved of their duties to their negligence and gross mishandling of the situation
i plan on calling management or hilton corporate offices after the holiday to lodge formal complaints
regardless we will never stay at this hotel again

i had an incredibly disappointing experience during my stay at this hilton club the central at th new york property which is particularly disheartening as a diamond member who rarely shares negative feedback
while the hotel itself was decent — especially the lobby bathrooms all the bathrooms are equipped with bidets — my experience was marred by significant issues
upon arrival i was greeted by buckets placed throughout the lobby catching water from a leaking ceiling
this unexpected situation led to a halt in checkins
although we waited two hours for the water to be restored we soon discovered that there was no hot water available for our showers
for two days my family and i endured freezing cold showers despite being repeatedly assured that the issue was resolved and they were waiting for the boilers to heat up
i was visiting nyc with my family but my daughter fell very ill after taking freezing cold showers so we were stuck in this dungeon for most of the trip and had to order medicine to help her recover
the lack of communication from the staff was particularly frustrating we only learned about the hot water problem by stepping into the cold shower ourselves
on the third day hot water returned but it was scalding hot — so much so that it felt like boiling water
to add to our difficulties the elevator broke down later that same day
when it came time to check out on the fourth day i had to make three phone calls just to receive a copy of my bill via email
given these numerous issues i cannot recommend this property
i will unequivocally refrain from returning unless points are offered in exchange for this review
i believe my experience warrants a serious reconsideration of management practices at this location
the hotel should change its name to hilton club the central motel — because that’s essentially what it is
there’s no food service of any kind no stocked minibar aside from two bottles of water and just one ice machine for the entire hotel
checkout is am which is definitively a first
during our stay there was no hot water in the shower for two full days
for a night you expect a lot more
definitely avoid

i must say we were impressed the room was beautiful and with the ph we had a fantastic view everyone mentions the heated toilet seat and bidet and of course i must too what a nice added feature but we also really enjoyed the power blinds and even the master switch at the door so you dont have to turn everything off individually before you leave the room

on the first night when we realized we did not have bedding for the sofa bed we called to the front desk and not only did they send it right up but they sent someone up to make up the bed for us
this was super helpful and so much nicer than the normal quick knock and dum of a pile of bedding into your arms at most hotels
the only reason i did not give stars is for two reasons
first is that we arrived pretty early in the day around am and as expected our room was not close to ready
no problem we expected that
we checked in left our bags will bell services and off we went to explore
when we came back around pm with two highly exhausted girls however our room was still not ready

of course check in is not until pm so technically it was not late but i guess i figured since we had been there so early that perhaps our room could have bene prioritized this is something i dont understand about hotels
they will have rooms cleaned that sit empty for hours until the guests arrive while in some other cases the guests are there early and they are made to wait
so we had to wait in their interior courtyard which was very cute and our room was finally ready at pm
second reason for stars versus relates to trash pickup
staying here as an owner we do not get daily housekeeping which we are fine with
but on the second day i texted down to the front desk to ask what we should do with our trash that was piling up
i was told to just leave it outside our room
so we put it out there assuming it would be picked up soon
hours later it was still sitting outside our room in the hallway
for such a nice place this just seems a bit unseemly
so they could have been better about that
overall its probably nit picking
we really did have a great time and would stay there again
location is fantastic rooms are nice and staff is exceptional if they could just be better about trash

our stay at hilton wilmington christiana had its ups and downs
upon arrival we were greeted warmly by the reception staff and promptly checked in even a little early which set a positive tone for our stay
the hotel offered a pleasant ambiance the common areas were clean and the lobby was wellappointed
parking was ample and the location was convenient offering easy access to local attractions shopping areas and the venue where we attended a wedding during our stay
our initial room appeared wellmaintained and comfortable
we did request a room with a lanai which was lovely but a room next to the pool can be noisy
the real problem arose when we attempted to take a shower
the water temperature fluctuated constantly oscillating between scalding and freezing
we contacted the front desk twice and they were quick to respond
however the first technician insisted that the water was at a consistent temperature and we simply didn’t let the water warm up long enough
on the second call another technician named stephen validated our complaint and offered us a room change without hesitation

it is always an inconvenience to have to switch rooms in the middle of a stay but the second room did provide a respite from the inconsistent water temperatures albeit now we had to endure the traffic noise from the other side of the hotel
despite this issue the hotel staff remained friendly and attentive and the other aspects of our stay were satisfactory
we also really enjoyed the market bar and restaurant
my only other complaint would be that for the price nearly for a night stay breakfast should have been included
overall it has potential but improvements are needed to justify the cost
computers down when we arrived and credit card information was written down on a pink note pad along with the driver’s license

during my recent stay at the hotel i encountered a few aspects that left me with mixed feelings
firstly the ac in the room was set at a fixed temperature of degrees and unfortunately it couldnt be adjusted to a colder setting
as someone who prefers a cooler environment this was a disappointment
its important for hotels to cater to varying temperature preferences to ensure optimal comfort for all guests
in addition to the ac issue the room itself felt somewhat dated
while it provided the basic amenities needed for a comfortable stay the overall decor and furnishings could benefit from a modern touch to enhance the ambiance and create a more inviting atmosphere
moving on to the conference room which happened to be located in the basement it too shared the same dated aesthetic as the guest room
furthermore the lighting in the conference room was noticeably low creating a somewhat dim and less productive environment
considering its basement location introducing natural lighting or improving the overall lighting system would greatly enhance the functionality and appeal of the conference room
on a positive note i must commend the staff for their pleasant and accommodating demeanor
their friendly and helpful nature added a positive touch to my overall experience at the hotel
their professionalism and willingness to assist guests are commendable qualities that significantly contribute to creating a welcoming atmosphere

in summary while there were certain areas that could benefit from improvement such as the inability to adjust the room temperature dated room decor and lackluster conference room lighting the hotel staffs pleasant demeanor served as a redeeming factor
its crucial for hotels to continuously evaluate and update their facilities to meet the evolving needs and expectations of their guests
if you prioritize a cool room temperature a modern ambiance and welllit conference spaces it may be worth considering alternative options
however if friendly and helpful staff is a priority for you this hotel might still provide a satisfactory stay
ultimately its important for hotels to address these areas for improvement to ensure a more enjoyable experience for their guests

the location of the hilton club west th street is very good
first and foremost in relation to the wonderful central park but also in relation to e
subway short distance to shopping on th avenue for example
the room we had a king studio on the th floor with a view of th street was also very good

very good and comfortable beds including pillows and mattresses

as i see it the cleaning staff did the best job and gave the best impression
they were both good and nice all credit to them
another advantage of this hotel was that there were never problems with crowded elevators slow elevators and long waits for elevators as you often find in more traditional hotels in nyc
however the lobby area was not particularly inviting and pleasant
the lobby area was small and cramped and no seating where it was possible to sit secluded to relax or to do a little work
but what was most disappointing about the hotel was the general level of service and customer service from the reception staff
my assessment is that the level of this important element is that it was far below what i expect and what i am used to
i have stayed at other more traditional hotels nearby and further down towards times square where the level of service was of a slightly different caliber
not least i have stayed at other hilton hotels especially on the west coast where the customer service was at a significantly higher level
it started already at checkin where they initially were unable to find my booking this was sorted out relatively quickly
when i as politely as i could asked if it was possible to get a nice room a little higher up the floors i received answer that all our rooms are nice

i mean when you are going to stay at the hotel for a week and pay a significant amount of money for just that it must be possible to at least try to be a little nice to the guests how difficult is it to say “thank you for choosing to stay here with us have a wonderful week here at hilton club”
a little more focus on customer service and maybe a few more hours on basic staff training would hardly hurt
another small challenge that i experienced was about my wish for a late checkout on the day of departure
we are talking about hours extra time beyond the normal checkout time
this was anything but easy even though i of course made it clear that i would pay for these extra hours
i also reffered to the hotels own policies where it was written in black and white that late checkout is possible
but anyway this was sorted out in a good way in the end
but not without the hotel manager having to decide about it
the manager was nice and as mentioned this was resolved
but i think this is one of very few times in my long hotel career as a guest that i have experienced late checkout as a real problem
but to summarize the room and location were great the cleaning staff were both nice and good but beyond that i feel that hilton club west th is lacking to much in other areas
therefore it is important to be aware that hilton club is basically a slightly different concept than what you may be used to from other more traditional hotels
this is important to be aware of before you book this hotel
i for one had not done my homework here i had not done enough research beforehand
therefore there will probably be no return to the hilton club on my next trip to nyc unless the hotel management convinces me otherwise

i stayed at the hilton club west th in nyc
i was very impressed

i did not purchase at this time due to conflicting priorities however i was satisfied with the information shared and it’s still a consideration for a later time
i loved that this building was located on billionaire’s row in manhattan
it’s within walking distance to many attractions like carnegie hall many luxury stores the museum of modern art central park and many places to eat
the building is very well kept and everyone was friendly

my room was on the th floor and had great views from the floor to ceiling windows
this review may not be applicable for everyone but i think it is important to share because others may have or be able to avoid a similar experience
my partner and i were considering purchasing a timeshare with hilton
we spoke with someone when we were staying in hawaii and purchased a travel plan to see how the program works

we were told by the person who sold the program to us and received a commission that the timing could be flexible and not to worry about the expiration on the contract because customer service could extend the time for us easily
i was concerned because the timing of expiration on the contract aligned with when i was scheduled to take the bar examination after completing law school
we really wanted this trip as a celebration for completing law school and the bar
i vocalized this to the agent and they assured me it would not be a problem

however once the contract was signed and we spoke with the customer service agents over the phone no longer the people trying to sell us something we were told rather coldly that we would have to book the trip before the expiration time or forfeit the money
we were forced to book a whirlwind trip here in february weeks before i am scheduled to take the bar examination or lose the money we contributed to this program
we were forced to go to new york because this program only allows for a three day trip at this one location even though they advertise dozens of other locations
we previously stayed in cabo for our first trip on this program which allows for a total of days collectively for the credit purchased
we stayed in cabo for four days and only had three days left under our program
then we were told we could only stay in new york because that is the only location that allows for three day stays
absolute scam
we were and are extremely upset with hilton and their abysmal customer service but decided to make the most of the trip rather than forfeit the money we already spent
however it was clear upon arrival that we were not high on the priority list
we already had our pitch regarding the possibility of purchasing a timeshare in cabo which we declined
our second stay in new york had no pitch scheduled and once the staff realized we were not a possible commission the service became laughable
also we came when it was not busy yet somehow ended up in probably one of the worst rooms possible a courtyard room on the fourth floor right next to the elevator
also this view of the court yard is an extremely generous statement
upon checking in i asked about the rooftop courtyard and the receptionist responded thats for owners with a penthouse only and made me feel like i was an idiot for even asking
even though there are dozens of pictures on these reviews and the website showing a rooftop courtyard that looks public
so do not expect to be looking out at the city like in their picture advertisements the view from our stay will likely be more indicative of your future experience
needless to say we will not be returning
i spent days at this hotel and received world class service
shout out to sheila reception and avi door man
they were so gracious and wonderful that we want to take them on our next vacation

i recently stayed at the doubletree by hilton hotel newark airport and experienced a very concerning issue with the water in my room
both the hot and cold water from the sink and shower had a strong musty moldlike odor
the smell was not faint — it was immediately noticeable from several feet away as soon as the water was turned on
it was overpowering enough that it made using the sink or shower uncomfortable
to make sure it wasn’t just the drain i performed a simple test by filling a cup with water and taking it into another room
the water itself still had a mild stagnantmusty smell when smelled up close though not as overpowering as when running from the tap
this suggests a plumbing or maintenance issue rather than just a surface drain odor
this was not a sulfur or “rotten egg” smell — it was more like the smell of water left sitting in a bottle for days
regardless of the cause this is not what guests expect from a hiltonbranded property
i am not sure whether this is isolated to my room or a broader building issue but it raises concerns about maintenance and sanitation standards
i would strongly encourage hotel management to investigate the plumbing system and ensure that guest rooms are being properly maintained

i recently stayed one night at the doubletree by hilton hotel newark airport while in town for a concert at the prudential center
while there were some positive aspects to this stay there were also several issues including a serious safety concern that i feel are worth sharing
positives
checkin was smooth and the lobby felt bright open and clean
the breakfast was excellent honestly one of the best i’ve had at any doubletree
even with a large crowd on a busy sunday morning the dining staff handled everything efficiently
the fitness center was clean and the pool area was large and welllit
i did not use the pool but it did look very fun
elevators were fast and i never had to wait long
concerns
upon entering my room i discovered a broken piece of metal lodged in the outlet near the bed a serious safety hazard especially for families with kids
the mini fridge was stuck shut and i didn’t want to force it open for fear of being put at fault for the unusable fridge
the blinds were stained and the windows were discolored with brown residue
the door frame was damaged which gave the room a worn poorly maintained feel
the thermostat was stuck at °f and couldn’t be adjusted leaving the room uncomfortably chilly
unfortunately my experience at checkout was disappointing
despite standing in the designated diamondgold priority line i was passed over multiple times while others in the regular line were helped ahead of me
i was never acknowledged or greeted until another guest in line invited me to go ahead
it was disheartening to feel ignored especially as a loyal hilton diamond member and multiple staff at the front desk
i still appreciate the hardworking staff and the positive aspects of the property but this stay fell short of the standard i’ve come to expect from hilton
i hope these issues especially the safety hazard are addressed for the sake of future guests
i tried to contact the hotel directly about the concerns and was left in silence after days
as i do not have kids i felt fine in the room but otherwise i would not find this to be an acceptable or safe stay
i would consider looking elsewhere for my future stays in newark considering the radio silence i was dealt when trying to privately deal with my concerns
all photos are attached to this review including the stained window and blinds and the metal stuck in the outlet

instead there was no hot water
i was barely able to wash up and i couldn’t even wash my hair

i called the same day after checking out while i was still in the area about minutes away but no one answered the lost and found line

instead of checking the room they only checked the lost and found area and seemed uninterested in actually helping

we visited the hilton parsippany for a pop culture convention so this review is about that experience not the rooms or the accommodations
as a convention venue the hilton parsippany does a fantastic job with the space
being a hotel the layout is very open modern and easily navigable
of course plenty of parking too
the layout of the meeting rooms provides plenty of space for vendors celebrity photo ops and the hoards of people attending the event
while the areas do fill up with shoppers and people trying to get from point a to point b it all moves rather well
attending a convention regardless of size at the hilton parsippany is a great experience
navigating where you are and where you need to be is a breeze
the hilton already has plenty of signage for the various rooms and the convention added additional signage specific to their event
there is plenty of lighting and high ceilings in most of the spaces
the hotel staff keeps the communal areas clean and swept

i am currently staying at hilton dubai the walk and the stay overall has been comfortable and enjoyable
the rooms are spacious clean and wellmaintained providing a relaxing environment for families
however the main concern during this stay has been the breakfast and dinner arrangements at hilton dubai jumeirah
as guests from hilton the walk are directed there for meals the dining areas become significantly congested especially during peak hours
finding a table can be difficult queues are often long and the overall experience feels crowded rather than relaxing
the situation clearly reflects a gap in planning and resource allocation
while the staff are extremely polite professional and working very hard they appear understaffed for the volume of guests being handled
this puts unnecessary pressure on them and impacts the guest experience

the administration should take a more proactive role in managing this—either by reopening additional dining facilities at hilton the walk or by increasing staffing and capacity at hilton jumeirah during busy periods
better crowd management and operational planning would greatly improve the overall experience
in summary while the rooms and staff are excellent the congestion during breakfast and dinner needs urgent attention from management to match the standards expected from the hilton brand

hilton the walk is a rare example of a hotel that truly lives up to its prestigious reputation
the first thing that strikes you is the incredible view from the windows—a breathtaking panorama that defines the entire atmosphere of your stay
for food enthusiasts the hotel offers access to multiple worldclass restaurants
each venue impresses with a vast variety of dishes stunning scenery and remarkably fast service
the location is a strategic masterpiece for any traveler
staying here allows you to completely bypass the need for taxis or public transport
everything you might need—from the beach to premium shopping—is within easy walking distance
the service is beyond reproach
i want to highlight eloisa whose professionalism is unmatched she handles every request with incredible speed
the whatsapp concierge service is another standout feature providing instant support and a hasslefree experience
whether you are here for business or a family vacation this hotel is a perfect choice
the only minor drawback is the elevator wait time during busy periods but in the grand scheme of things it’s negligible
a solid experience

we faced a disappointing experience during our recent stay
we had booked one of their penthouses for a weekend getaway with my sisters and our children but upon checkin our apartment was not ready
unfortunately there were no apologies offered instead the manager informed us that it was not her fault and she couldnt guarantee when it would be ready
she suggested an alternative room which she referred to as complimentary although we had already paid for our booking making the term confusing in this context
while we understand that issues can arise during busy periods the customer service and recovery efforts did not meet the standards expected from hilton
it was disheartening to experience what felt like a lack of professionalism and understanding
at one point we felt that we were not being treated unfairly and the offer of minimal service followed by a gesture of free food as compensation which we declined did not address our concerns
our primary issue was not just the inconvenience but also the impression that the situation was not a priority for habiba
however we later spoke with ayman who listened to our concerns and offered a sincere apology which was appreciated
we are considering returning to see if this was an isolated incident with habiba or if it reflects a broader issue with the brands service in the uae

despite being a hilton honors member i faced multiple issues during checkin
after arriving from the airport i was given a room different from what i had booked
when i got to the room i was surprised to find it was not a king bed as reserved but two separate beds
i tried calling the front desk but no one answered
exhausted from traveling i went back down to reception and they apologized saying they had no room available matching my booking
frankly in serviced apartments or professionally managed properties when this happens the guest is usually upgraded—not downgraded
and that wasn’t the only problem
i accepted the room temporarily because i was completely exhausted
when i used the bathroom water started leaking everywhere
i called again—this time they answered—and i explained that the situation was unacceptable as the water spread throughout the room due to very poor plumbing quality
after that i was moved to another room that matched my original booking—despite being told just five minutes earlier that it wasn’t available
they apologized for this disaster and the lack of professionalism by offering breakfast which i neither wanted nor could use since i don’t wake up in time for it

if you need basic amenities ear plugs a toothbrush or any essential item you are told to call—but when you call no one answers

i had booked a room for a night stay in dubai

my wife and i were celebrating our th wedding anniversary during the appbased checkin process i selected a room which unfortunately was not available when we reached the hotel because the guests staying therein had requested a late checkout
regardless the hotel staff arranged for an alternate room on the same floor with superb views of downtown dubai and the creek
before giving us the room the hotel staff decorated the room for our milestone anniversary and also baked us a cake gestures we truly appreciate
the king studio room was immaculate and came with a kitchen complete with a seater round dining table stove oven refrigerator and a sink
the room also housed a washerdryer which we did not use
additionally there was ample storage space for our four large bags
the furniture and bed in the room were cozy and comfortable
the staff responsible for room cleaning were professional and courteous maintaining the room in excellent condition
on the other hand the water pressure in the bathroom was quite low making the rain shower head and the handheld shower essentially anemic
a feature that we found somewhat odd was the placement of the flat screen tv in the middle of the room between the bed and sofa effectively serving as a divider
perhaps the tv could have been wallmounted but this was not a major concern since we were mostly out and about in the city
regardless i tried connecting my phone to the tv using the cast function but was unsuccessful
i am tech savvy person but the interface on the tv to successfully cast content left a lot to be desired
ive stayed in many hilton hotels but never experienced such an inability to cast content from my devices
next comes the food while we did not eat in the hotels only restaurant the morning breakfast spread was quite sumptuous
there was the usual continental fare including breads cheeses and juices in addition to local dishes such as hummus and pita tabouleh salad olives and feta a soup with fava beans and the like
since we are vegetarian we did not check out the meats and such
on one of the days we were there the breakfast buffet included poha an indian staple which was wellprepared and served hot
further the kitchen could also prepare additional items from a limited selection
one of the days we ordered aloo parantha and on another day we ordered idli and vada with sambhar
the aloo parantha was wellmade but the idlivada was just passable
overall our stay was comfortable restful and relaxing

the hotel staff including riki at the reception ahmed in guest services and malika guest services ambassador were eager to help us and make sure we felt welcomed i want to convey my sincere thanks to the entire hotel staff and particularly to riki ahmed and malika

we chose this hotel and booked a twobedroom apartment for our business trip of three people
first of all we would like to express our gratitude to riki the front desk receptionist
since we were unsure about the end time of our trip we had to extend our stay on short notice several times
he was extremely patient in rearranging rooms for us to minimize any inconvenience and his service was both professional and thoughtful
we particularly liked the twobedroom threebed unit
it featured a spacious wraparound balcony and an oversized living room which delivered an excellent living experience and stunning views
we also want to give a big thumbsup to the housekeeping and room service team—they always delivered our requested items promptly no matter the time of day
if we were to point out any inconveniences there would be three aspects
first we were not fond of the shower setup in the room which requires you to stand inside the bathtub while bathing to our mind this is neither very safe nor hygienic
second this is a very personal observation but the perfume scent in the hotel lobby was quite strong and overpowering which we found hard to get used to
third some rooms lack a desk lamp leaving no welllit space for working especially if you do not wish to work at the dining table
overall we were quite satisfied with our stay and would be happy to choose this hotel again if we have the chance

during my recent stay at the hilton dubai creek hotel residence i was impressed from the very beginning
before arriving i received a pleasant welcome email providing helpful information about the property and its facilities which i found useful
on arrival i was greeted by the friendly team in the unusual parking area entrance which felt a little different but worked well
i made my way to reception on the ground floor and was again welcomed and supported by the team
check in was quick and efficient and before long i was on my way to my room
on arrival i was pleasantly surprised to find a lovely apartment style room with a nice balcony overlooking the creek and hotel pool
the apartment included a kitchen and dining area a separate bedroom and a separate bathroom and toilet with all the usual amenities
the property offers the usual hilton facilities including a nice restaurant shisha bar spa gym and a relaxing pool area with a bar available too
there was also a happy hour in the restaurant for hilton loyalty members which was a nice touch
it is worth noting the hotel is just over a year old and the unique entrance can create some confusion for taxi drivers
i found that walking to the hotel next door helped as it has a more traditional hotel entrance which made it easier for drivers to locate
the residence is part of a lovely development on the creekside and the newly opened supermarket close by was ideal for picking up essentials or for a simple meal in the apartment
the location is convenient for both the airport and the original parts of dubai which makes it a great base for exploring the city
overall i really enjoyed my stay the team were helpful throughout and the property was always clean and well maintained
i look forward to visiting again in the future

i chose the doubletree by hilton birmingham perimeter park because i typically trust the hilton brand
this stay did not meet even basic expectations
from the moment we arrived the condition of the property raised concerns
the parking lot was visibly unkempt with trash present and there is a posted sign stating “park at your own risk” with no liability for loss or damage
at the same time guests are required to pay for parking
charging for parking without any visible security patrol or controlled access is difficult to justify
during checkin i accidentally left my wallet at the front desk
after getting to our room i realized it was missing and called the front desk multiple times with no answer
we went back downstairs and the clerk confirmed he had my wallet
while i was relieved it was there what followed was unacceptable
he had my wallet knew exactly who i was and what room i was in yet made no effort to contact me
i then had to ask him directly if he saw the incoming calls
his response was that he did see them and chose not to answer
there was no explanation and no urgency
given the lack of communication i had no choice but to assume the worst and immediately cancel my cards to protect myself
that added unnecessary stress and disruption to what should have been a routine checkin experience
we were also given incorrect information at checkin regarding breakfast
we were told it was included but after eating on our second morning we were informed it was not
to her credit a staff member stepped in and handled the situation professionally

the room itself showed signs of poor maintenance including visible stains on the carpet
the tv connection was weird
always disconnected from the service and multiple remotes

i really wanted to enjoy my stay here but there were several issues that made the experience frustrating
let’s start with the biggest problems • we were never informed at checkin that housekeeping wasn’t standard
even after multiple requests for daily room cleaning no one ever came
• we never received new towels — we had to physically go to the front desk to get them
• the tv volume was awful — i suspect the equalizer was broken
• none of the ice machines worked on any floor we tried
• i paid for the breakfast package yet every morning i had to prove i had it to a visibly annoyed attendant
there was no guest list available to make this easier
• the buffet area had a leak during breakfast — not what you’d expect from a hilton property
• the lobby snacks were wildly overpriced — for a single hershey bar is excessive
• the front desk staff seemed undertrained
they were often confused about how to fulfill basic requests
• a room service cart sat outside our door every day which wouldn’t be a big deal if our room was ever serviced
• i requested a luggage rack and it never came
• our trash was never taken out
i ended up taking the full bins myself to staff and asking them to replace the liners
the room was clean upon checkin which i appreciated and we got a free warm cookie
the location is great close to restaurants and just a short drive to downtown
but overall the lack of followthrough poor service coordination and subpar experience for the price really took away from what could’ve been a pleasant stay
this hotel has potential but it’s clear there are operational issues that need serious attention

i stayed at the kelly birmingham tapestry collection by hilton while in town for a major careerrelated event january th th and unfortunately this was one of the most disappointing hotel experiences i’ve had
i arrived around pm—nearly two hours after checkin time—and was told my room had not yet been cleaned and i would need to wait
i waited approximately minutes during which time several guests who arrived after me were checked in immediately and escorted to their rooms
the desk attendant even offered for us to sit at the bar for drinks we declined as our time was limited
this delay put me behind schedule for an important event
after expressing my frustration the front desk attendant offered a breakfast credit that required charging items to the room and trusting they would be credited back after checkout
i declined and asked for a manager
i was told the manager would be available the next day
when i finally entered the room—the one i was told “needed to be cleaned”—it was clearly not clean
there was hair in the bathtub on the sink and on the bathroom floor
the showertubtoilet leaked continuously leaving standing water between the shower and toilet for the entirety of my stay
the carpet was sticky in places turning the soles of our socks and feet black
the room temperature was degrees upon entry while it was degrees outside and the heat took an unreasonable amount of time to come on
we did call to the front desk to report the issue they advised to give it a few minutes and if it did not come on they would send someone up
it came on about minutes later
because of the issue with the room at check in i was late to my event
the next morning i again asked to speak with a manager and was told no manager was on site
the desk clerk who was kind and professional offered a credit asked me to leave my contact information and assured me the manager would call me monday
no one called monday
or tuesday
i finally called the hotel on wednesday and was directed to kenyatta white
initially she seemed empathetic and stated that the situation should have been handled before reaching her
she told me she would process an additional refund and add hilton honors points and i specifically requested a detailed email outlining the refund and points
she assured me it would be sent immediately
it was not
when i called back on thursday january ms
white had a noticeably smug and dismissive attitude
she claimed we had already discussed the refund and points and did not know what the issue i had was because it had been handled
the “refund” unknown to me was the original credit from the front desk the day of my checkout not anything additional
she did not tell me how many points were added and coldly instructed me to check hiltonhonors
com myself
to make matters worse the points she claimed were added on january st did not appear until i followed up again on january nd after my call with mrs
white and while i was writing this review
the lack of transparency and followthrough is extremely disappointing
i do feel i should have been compensated greater for this inconvenience
my career and reputation was jeopardized due to a lack of coordination with this hotel
this entire experience—from checkin to room conditions to management’s handling of the situation to fruition—shows a serious lack of organization and accountability
i now understand why hilton properties are no longer my first choice and i would not recommend this hotel to anyone traveling for an important event

stayed at the hilton in alpharetta for a work trip and overall had a solid experience with just a few areas that could use improvement
i arrived late—as usual—and checkin went smoothly
the front desk agent was extremely polite and helpful

i asked for a plastic key i always do this as a backup in case the app fails and while she was processing it another employee not front desk maybe engineering are maintenance chimed in to say i didn’t need to visit the desk if i checked in on the app
i had literally just explained why i came down so that felt a bit dismissive
either way i got my keys she gave me two and headed up
my hilton gold status wasn’t acknowledged—not a big deal but it’s usually part of the routine
my room was on the th floor and looked great
just inside the door was a cuisinart coffee maker dualcup a small refrigerator with two complimentary water bottles and a low countertop with two drawers
between the coffee area and the closet was a wall mirror
the closet itself had hangers an ironing board iron and a safe
at the top of the closet there was what looked like a concealed compartment—unclear what its for but it stood out

the bedroom had a wallmounted tv above a second set of drawers with an ice bucket and glasses a desk with a nonrolling alligator print chair surprisingly stylish and a plush armchair with a side table and lamp
bedside setup included two lamps an alarm clock and a limited number of usb ports and plugs
outlets were scarce overall—one near the coffee maker but halfblocked and one in the hallway
the bathroom was clean with a glass shower lighted mirror and a builtin nightlight which i always appreciate
towels were average—not luxury but not thin or scratchy
a hair dryer was provided
the door had a solid privacy guard at the bottom
i didn’t have much of a view at night but the room felt comfortable and wellkept
breakfast was a bit of a mixed bag
the staff was great—super friendly and welcoming—but the food didn’t impress
the bacon was practically inedible hard like plastic
pork sausage links were decent and the gouda cheese grits were flavorful but salty
premade waffles were good
there were the usual extras like breads pastries and fruit but overall it didn’t feel like a standout hilton breakfast
i didnt go back after that for the rest of my stay
the snack shop by the front desk could use serious attention
it was heavily stocked with healthy options that didn’t seem to be selling while candy and chocolate were gone almost immediately
i had a late night chocolate craving and came up emptyhanded—only ice cream was available
on top of that they sell glass coke bottles that require a bottle opener but there’s none in the room or the shop
i had to go back to the front desk and ask the guy to use the one he carries in his pocket
that shouldn’t be how it works—either include a bottle opener in the room or offer one with the purchase
i didn’t request housekeeping during my stay but i liked that they left a card on my door letting me know i could reach out by pm if i needed anything
that’s a nice considerate touch i haven’t seen at other hiltons
parking is mostly in the rear
there are diamond and handicap spots up front but for most guests you’ll need to walk a bit either to the front entrance or the side door—even from the closest spot in the main lot
each evening the hotel had a lively vibe
the bar and patio areas had groups hanging out enjoying drinks and conversation
it seems like a fun place to stay if youre here for leisure
i was just in town for work so i didn’t get to take advantage of it—and i never even saw the pool if they have one
the night staff were friendly and attentive every time i passed through the lobby
overall the hotel is clean the staff is great and the room was comfortable
just a few tweaks—better breakfast execution a stocked snack shop and accessible bottle openers—would elevate the stay
positive negative floor was not vacuumed prior to our arrival
my stay was great the hotel was clean comfortable and fun
i loved the area—so much to see and do
would definitely recommend to others ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

about am the adjoining room came in and that’s when i realized the walls in this hotel are paper thin

eventually hotel staff came up because others also complained about the banging on the door

i woke up

review the starling atlanta midtown – great location disappointing rooms
the only real selling point of the starling is its location
it’s right in the middle of midtown — walkable to piedmont park colony square and plenty of restaurants and bars
if you want convenience it has that
beyond that it falls apart
the rooms are poorly designed and feel neglected
the décor tries to be bold and modern but comes off mismatched and cheaply executed
layout choices don’t make sense finishes feel worn and the overall impression is dated rather than stylish
cleanliness is the bigger issue
the room did not feel properly cleaned — dust stained surfaces and a general lack of attention to detail
bathrooms in particular felt grimy rather than fresh
for a hotel at this price point that’s unacceptable
the lobby and marketing suggest an upscale experience
the rooms do not deliver it
if location is your only priority it works
if you care about thoughtful design cleanliness and overall quality there are better options in midtown

if you want to experience the gatsby side of atlanta the candler hotel is an absolute masterpiece

originally built in by cocacola founder asa griggs candler this historic skyscraper has been meticulously restored into a boutique hotel that feels like a step back in time—but with all the star modern comforts
the architecture vibe
the moment you walk into the lobby the white georgia marble tiffany glass and original brass elevators take your breath away
it’s easily one of the most instagrammable spots in the city
even if you arent staying overnight the grand staircase is worth a visit alone
dining at by george
located in what used to be the central bank and trust the hotel restaurant by george is a destination in its own right
• the breakfast the tres leches french toast is legendary
• the dinner don’t miss the atlantic sea scallops or the steak and fries with cognac sauce
• the cocktails the bar program is toptier
try a classic martini—the atmosphere practically demands it
the rooms
the rooms strike a perfect balance high ceilings and large windows that flood the space with natural light paired with sleek contemporary furnishings
the bathrooms are spacious shoutout to the luxury robes and designer toiletries and provide a quiet sanctuary from the hustle of downtown
protips for your stay
history buffs ask about the basement vault
it famously housed the secret cocacola formula for years
location you are steps away from the peachtree center marta station making it easy to get to the airport or midtown without a car
valet like most downtown hotels valet is the way to go but be sure to call for your car – minutes early during peak hours
bottom line the candler is more than just a place to sleep it’s a piece of atlanta history
perfect for a romantic staycation or a sophisticated business trip

i stayed at the candler hotel in january as a hilton honors diamond member booking through american express hotel collection
the hotel itself is stunning—the historic restoration is remarkable the lobby popcorn is a nice touch and there were several instances of genuinely excellent customer service during my stay
however i experienced significant issues with benefit communication and management followup that required over two weeks to resolve
the problem
at checkin i was explicitly told by front desk staff that my amex hotel collection property credit could be used for valet parking and would stack with my diamond food beverage benefits
i relied on this information when incurring charges
at checkout i was informed for the first time of an undisclosed hotel policy requiring the amex credit to be exhausted before diamond benefits could apply
additionally only one of my two daily diamond fb credits was applied to my folio
when i raised these issues and requested correction the front desk declined to adjust the charges resulting in an balance that would not have existed had the policies been disclosed upfront
the bigger issue
i emailed the front office manager the same day as instructed by the front desk to request review
i received no response for days
i escalated to hilton guest assistance twice each time being promised a response within hours
neither promise was kept
i contacted american express hotel collection who called the hotel directly on my behalf
even they were unable to get the charge reversed

after days five emails two phone calls filing bbb complaints and initiating a credit card dispute the general manager finally responded and resolved the issue within hours—something that should have happened on day
resolution
to their credit once senior management became involved they were apologetic and provided appropriate compensation
the gm personally called to apologize and hiltons executive office followed up independently
recommendation
the candler is a beautiful property with genuine potential but there are serious training gaps around
accurate communication of benefit policies at checkin
management responsiveness to guest concerns
empowering front desk staff to correct billing errors
until they fix these training issues you may wish to avoid the hotel if you are a diamond member or booking through amex
i do hope they sort these training issues soon as the property itself is lovely and if possible i would love to make this my main hotel to stay at when i am visiting atlanta

my family and i recently had two stays at the hilton garden inn chattanoogahamilton place chosen for its convenient location as a halfway point on our travels
while the hotel offered some expected amenities several aspects of our experience left much to be desired particularly regarding customer service
front desk experience stars
unfortunately the most consistent and disappointing aspect of both stays was the demeanor of the man working the front desk
on both occasions his attitude was incredibly uninviting bordering on rude
during our first stay when we requested an extra towel as only three were provided for four guests i was told to pick it up myself
after a minute wait at the front desk with no one present my second attempt to retrieve the towel was met with an unhelpful and surly response

for our second visit i proactively requested an extra towel and two extra pillows at checkin only to be told that extra pillows might not be available – a surprising and frustrating response from a hotel
amenities breakfast stars
the pool was closed during our first stay which was an inconvenience for a family traveling with children though not a dealbreaker
it was open during our second visit
the breakfast which incurs an additional charge was described as okay
however no information or directions regarding breakfast were provided at checkin on either occasion requiring us to seek out details ourselves
room condition stars
we ended up in the exact same room for both stays
the room was generally fairly clean
however there was a noticeable and unpleasant stain on one of the chairs
given our previous interactions we opted not to address it with the front desk and simply avoided using the chair
customer service vs
hotel promise stars
the most ironic part of our stay was the presence of posters throughout the hotel assuring guests that staff were happy to help with anything needed to make their stay more enjoyable
this statement felt entirely disconnected from our actual experience where requests were met with reluctance or a poor attitude
conclusion

in summary if you are looking for an average hotel with a fairly clean room an okay breakfast you pay extra for and are prepared to be treated like an inconvenience throughout your stay the hilton garden inn chattanoogahamilton place might be for you
given the availability of other similarly priced or cheaper hotels in the area this hilton garden inn will unfortunately not be my first second or even third choice for future stays

extremely disappointing experience from hilton london metropole
this property markets itself as a five star hotel but in execution it functions as a solid four star at best
given the premium room rates of to per night the actual experience fell incredibly short of expectations
as a diamond member the arrival experience was disappointing
there was a lengthy wait at reception with no dedicated lane for top tier loyalty members
the front desk staff appeared aloof exhausted and entirely disengaged providing a remarkably cold and uninviting welcome
furthermore there was no welcome note or standard recognition of status upon entering the room
the room itself was acceptable offering a minor upgrade to a high floor with a balcony but preparation was entirely overlooked
despite spending three hours coordinating via the hilton app chat prior to arrival none of my basic requests for extra pillows water or coffee were fulfilled
the communication on the app was slow unhelpful and ultimately resulted in empty promises
having to repeatedly request these basic items after check in turned the stay into unnecessary work
the executive lounge was functional but highly underwhelming suffering from overcrowding and a very basic selection of food and beverages
ironically i have experienced far more sincere gracious hospitality at three star properties over the past year
while the stay ended without further incident the overall lack of warmth and poor execution means i will not be returning
for travelers looking for a genuine true luxury experience in london i strongly advise considering actual five star hotels nearby such as the wellesley knightsbridge
the contrast in staff professionalism room quality and overall execution makes it clear that this hilton property simply does not belong in the same luxury category

despite being appropriately dressed and behaving respectfully we were refused entry

what made matters worse was the handling of the complaint afterwards
different explanations were given including claims that we had been offered the opportunity to remove items of clothing which simply did not happen
the final response ultimately relied on “management discretion” while avoiding any meaningful acknowledgement of how the experience came across from a customer perspective
i fully appreciate that private venues have dress codes and reserve the right to refuse entry
however there is a significant difference between enforcing standards and making guests feel unwelcome because of respectful expressions of national identity
most disappointing of all was hilton corporate distancing itself from the matter entirely despite the venue operating within one of its flagship london hotels
for a hotel and venue positioning themselves as luxury hospitality brands i expected far better communication professionalism and customer care

my recent stay at london hilton on park lane was overall a thoroughly enjoyable experience deserving of a solid fourstar commendation
from the outset the hotel impresses with its prime position overlooking hyde park offering some of the most captivating views in london
there is an immediate sense of scale and energy upon arrival with a lobby that is both lively and wellappointed striking a balance between modern comfort and classic city hotel charm
the room itself was well presented clean and thoughtfully arranged
while not overly extravagant it delivered everything one would expect from a reputable establishment of this calibre
the true highlight however was undoubtedly the view — waking up to the london skyline added a certain refinement to the stay that elevated the overall experience
service throughout was professional and efficient
staff were courteous and accommodating particularly during checkin and within the communal areas
while there were moments where the pace felt slightly brisk — perhaps a reflection of the hotel’s popularity — it never detracted significantly from the overall comfort of the stay
facilities were equally commendable with the executive spaces and dining options offering a pleasant environment to unwind
the atmosphere carries a certain cosmopolitan vibrancy making it well suited for both business and leisure travellers
if one were to offer a minor critique it would be that certain aspects of the property feel a touch dated when compared to some of london’s newer luxury offerings
however this is more than offset by its location views and consistently reliable service
in summary the london hilton on park lane delivers a polished and dependable experience in one of the city’s most desirable locations
it may not seek to overwhelm with extravagance but it succeeds in providing comfort convenience and a quietly confident standard of hospitality

للأسف هذه ليست أول مرة أسكن في hilton london park lane، وقلت هذه المرة أعطيه فرصة ثانية، لكن ندمت جداً
موظفو اللوبي غير متعاونين أبداً، أسلوبهم سيئ ونفسياتهم دائماً سيئة
حتى المدراء لا يقدّمون أي مساعدة، وكل شخص يتهرّب من المسؤولية ويحوّلك على شخص آخر بدون أي حل فعلي
النظافة سيئة، الغرف لا تُنظَّف إلا بعد الطلب مرات كثيرة، وحتى الأشياء الأساسية مثل الماء لا يتم توفيرها إلا بعد إلحاح
بسبب عدم الراحة نهائياً، اضطررت في النهاية إلى تغيير حجزي وحجز فندق آخر
وفوق هذا كله، قام الفندق بتغيير سياسات الإلغاء وأخذوا مبالغ إضافية بدون أي مرونة أو تقدير للضيف
حتى الأكل تغيّر للأسوأ
في السابق كان الطعام لذيذاً، أما الآن فعندما أعود من الخارج وأنا جائعة وأطلب روم سيرفيس، يكون الأكل سيئ جداً، بدون طعم، وحتى الملح غير موجود—فعلياً أكل ماصخ ولا يُؤكل
تجربة مخيبة للآمال من جميع النواحي، ولا تليق باسم هيلتون أو بسعر الإقامة
لا أنصح به إطلاقاً
this was not my first stay at hilton london park lane and i decided to give the hotel a second chance which i truly regret
the lobby staff are extremely unhelpful with poor attitudes and no willingness to assist
even the managers offer no real support—everyone avoids responsibility and keeps sending you to someone else without any solution
housekeeping is very poor
rooms are not cleaned unless you ask repeatedly and even basic amenities like water are not provided unless you keep requesting them
due to the complete lack of comfort i eventually had to change my booking and move to another hotel
on top of that the hotel changed the cancellation policy and charged additional fees showing no flexibility or consideration
even the food quality has declined significantly
it used to be good in the past but now when i return hungry and order room service the food is very bad tasteless and not even properly seasoned—literally bland and unpleasant
a very disappointing experience overall
does not reflect the hilton name and is absolutely not worth the price
my stay from december to january was unfortunately not a very pleasant one
i had hoped to enjoy a nice new year’s break at the hotel
the property has a long history and although it has been renovated there were several comfort issues
the central air conditioning was extremely noisy and the windows did not insulate well against the cold or wind
the room felt slightly warmer only when the curtains were fully closed
a portable heater was provided but the room was still cold unless the noisy air conditioning was left on
there also appeared to be an air deodorising device in the room which produced a highpitched mechanical sound and emitted a strong disinfectantlike smell that was quite unpleasant
in addition possibly due to ageing plumbing there was occasionally an unpleasant odour from the bathroom if the toilet lid was left open
the room was on a low floor
although described as facing hyde park the actual view was mainly rooftops of nearby buildings
the room was positioned in a narrow gap in this buildings meaning there was very little privacy — with the curtains open and lights on it was easy to see into neighbouring rooms and vice versa
unfortunately i also left behind a new limitededition white hello kitty hair band which recently purchased during another trip at checkout
i contacted the hotel within a few hours but it could not be located despite several followups
as it was white and left on the bed it may have been overlooked during housekeeping
none of these issues were the fault of the hotel staff
the hotel was extremely busy over the new year period and there were no alternative rooms available
hilton of course cannot be compared with fivestar or ultraluxury brands and perhaps my expectations were too high to begin with
that said you do get what you pay for
the pricing is relatively reasonable for the area and the location is excellent
from a valueformoney perspective it is still a workable option if budget and location are the main priorities
excellent welcome from zubair incredibly helpful and a real credit to your team
